_source: Insurance adjuster licensing materials, Xactimate estimation software, Property damage assessment guides, Auto collision repair standards
_note: Long-tail insurance claims knowledge. Property damage assessment, Xactimate estimating, auto collision evaluation, liability investigation, claims handling.

loss_assessment.initial_inspection: Document everything with photos/video before cleanup; measure affected areas; identify source of loss; determine scope of damage; note pre-existing damage separately

water_damage.categories: Cat 1 clean water (supply line break); Cat 2 gray water (washing machine, dishwasher); Cat 3 black water (sewage, flooding); category affects restoration cost and method
water_damage.classes: Class 1 (minimal absorption, <10% area); Class 2 (significant, carpet/walls <24 inches); Class 3 (ceiling, walls >24 inches, insulation); Class 4 (specialty drying, hardwood, plaster)
water_damage.mitigation: Emergency services within 24-48 hours; extract water, set dehumidifiers/air movers; monitor moisture levels with meter; prevent mold growth (appears 24-72 hours); dry to <15% moisture content
water_damage.coverage_issues: Sudden and accidental = covered; gradual seepage/maintenance = excluded; check if water damage or ensuing mold covered; flood requires separate policy

fire_damage.smoke_types: Wet smoke (low heat, smoldering, sticky residue, pungent); Dry smoke (high heat, fast-burning, powdery, easier to clean); Protein smoke (kitchen fires, invisible, strong odor)
fire_damage.scope: Structural damage to framing, roof, walls; contents damage (smoke, heat, water from firefighting); smoke odor permeates porous materials; need thermal imaging for hidden damage
fire_damage.restoration: Board-up and tarping immediate; structural engineer if compromised; demo fire-damaged materials; clean smoke residue; seal surfaces; ozone treatment or hydroxyl generators for odor; contents cleaning or pack-out

wind_hail_damage.roof_inspection: Shingle damage (missing, lifted, creased, granule loss); flashing damage; ridge cap; penetrations (vents, chimneys); use ladder, walk roof if safe or drone; photos with EXIF data
wind_hail_damage.hail_damage: Impact marks on shingles (bruising, divots, loss of granules in circular pattern); test hits (self-seal strip damage); soft metals (flashing, vents, gutters show dings); must have matching damage pattern across roof
wind_hail_damage.siding_damage: Vinyl cracks, dents; wood splits; aluminum dings; distinguish storm damage from age/wear; document with close-up photos
wind_hail_damage.claim_pitfalls: Cosmetic vs functional damage (cosmetic may not be covered or full replacement); aging roof (ACV vs RCV payment); wind-driven rain requires wind damage to opening first

estimating_xactimate.sketch: Draw floor plan to scale; measure perimeter (laser measure or tape); room dimensions; ceiling heights; note openings (doors, windows); elevation walls for height measurements; roof sketch (pitch, squares, complexity)

line_items.structure: Demo (R&R = remove and replace, RMV = remove only); framing (LF linear feet, SF square feet, EA each); drywall (SF with waste factor 10-15%); insulation (SF); flooring (SF + waste); paint (SF walls + ceiling)
line_items.contents: Furniture, electronics, clothing; research RCV (replacement cost value); apply depreciation for ACV (actual cash value); total loss if repair > RCV or restoration not feasible
line_items.labor_materials: Xactimate prices regional (zip code); includes labor and materials for most line items; overhead and profit (O&P) for contractor (10% each = 20% total) if 3+ trades involved

depreciation.straight_line: Useful life method; example: roof 20-year life, 10 years old = 50% depreciated; ACV = RCV × (remaining life / total life)
depreciation.per_year: Fixed % per year; carpet 10% per year (10-year life); flooring 5% per year (20-year life); appliances 10-20% per year
depreciation.recoverable: ACV paid up front; depreciation held back; after repairs complete, policyholder submits invoices, receives depreciation (recoverable depreciation); if don't repair, keep ACV only

overhead_profit.when_applicable: General contractor needed (3+ trades); large loss requiring coordination; not for small repairs or single-trade jobs
overhead_profit.calculation: 10% overhead + 10% profit on subtotal (not including O&P itself); some carriers allow on all labor/materials, others only contractor-performed items
overhead_profit.dispute: Common point of contention; document need for GC (complexity, multiple trades, scheduling); some states mandate O&P payment if required

policy_types.ho3_special: Most common homeowners; dwelling replacement cost (open peril - all risks except exclusions); personal property ACV (named peril - only listed causes); 16 named perils for contents
policy_types.ho5_comprehensive: Premium policy; both dwelling and contents replacement cost; open peril for both; broader coverage
policy_types.ho6_condo: Walls-in coverage; condo association covers building; HO-6 covers interior improvements, contents, loss assessment
policy_types.dp3_rental: Dwelling policy; landlord; covers structure; no contents (landlord's property separate); can add loss of rents coverage

coverage_limits.dwelling: Coverage A; amount to rebuild (not market value); should be replacement cost estimate; increased cost of construction if updated codes require upgrades
coverage_limits.other_structures: Coverage B; 10% of dwelling (typically); detached garage, shed, fence; separate limit
coverage_limits.personal_property: Coverage C; 50-70% of dwelling; contents; applies sublimits (jewelry $1,500, money $200, electronics $2,500); schedule high-value items separately
coverage_limits.loss_of_use: Coverage D; 20% of dwelling; additional living expenses (ALE); hotel, meals, storage while uninhabitable; actual expenses up to limit
coverage_limits.liability: Coverage E; $100k-$500k standard; covers legal liability for bodily injury or property damage to others; defense costs; umbrella policy adds $1M+
coverage_limits.medical_payments: Coverage F; $1k-$5k; pays medical bills for others injured on property regardless of fault; goodwill coverage

exclusions.common: Flood (separate policy through NFIP or private); earth movement (earthquake, landslide, sinkhole - separate earthquake policy); maintenance/wear and tear; intentional loss; war/nuclear
exclusions.water_damage_fine_print: Surface water, groundwater, sewer backup (can add rider) often excluded; but resulting damage may be covered (pipe bursts = covered, mold from long-term leak = excluded)
exclusions.mold: Limited or excluded; typically $10k sublimit if ensuing from covered loss (sudden pipe break); not covered if from maintenance issue (leaky roof for months)

collision_assessment.damage_inspection: Exterior panels (hoods, fenders, doors, bumpers); frame/unibody (measure for structural damage); suspension/steering; lights; glass; interior damage from impact
collision_assessment.total_loss_threshold: Varies by state (70-100% of ACV); if repair cost + salvage value > ACV, total loss; example: car worth $10k, repairs $8k, salvage $3k = $11k > $10k = total
collision_assessment.structural_damage: Frame or unibody damage; measure with laser or mechanical measuring system (Celette, Car-O-Liner); compare to factory specs; if >6mm out of spec, may be frame damage notation on title
collision_assessment.hidden_damage: Disassemble to find; suspension damage, subframe damage, cooling system; create supplement estimate for additional damage found during repair; 30-50% of estimates have supplements
collision_assessment.airbag_deployment: Airbags deployed = expensive ($1,000-3,000 per bag); SRS computer must be replaced; seat belts (pretensioners); steering wheel, dash; often pushes toward total loss

estimating_systems.ccc_mitchell_audatex: Auto estimating software; parts pricing databases; labor times from manufacturers or aftermarket guides; regional labor rates

parts_pricing.oem: Original Equipment Manufacturer; new parts from vehicle manufacturer; highest cost; best quality/fit; some policies require OEM
parts_pricing.aftermarket: Non-OEM new parts; certified (CAPA, NSF) or non-certified; 20-50% cheaper than OEM; quality varies; insurer can specify unless policy states OEM
parts_pricing.used_lkq: Like Kind and Quality; salvage yard parts; 50-70% of OEM cost; for older vehicles (5+ years); must match year, make, model; warranty varies
parts_pricing.reconditioned: Remanufactured; common for mechanical parts (engines, transmissions); core charge (return old part)

labor_operations.labor_rate: Regional; $40-100 per hour body labor; $80-150 mechanical; higher in metro areas; insurer database rates vs shop rates (negotiate)
labor_operations.overlap: Some operations overlap (remove bumper required for multiple repairs); estimating software calculates; don't pay full time for overlapping removal/installation
labor_operations.paint_materials: Separate from labor; paint, clear coat, reducer, hardener; calculated by refinish time × material rate ($30-60 per hour typically)

diminished_value.inherent_dv: Loss of value due to accident history; even after perfect repair, vehicle worth less; third-party claim (other party's insurance) must pay in most states; first-party (your insurance) varies
diminished_value.calculation: 17c formula (Georgia): ACV × 10% × damage modifier (0-1.0) × mileage modifier (0-1.0); or appraisal-based; typical 10-30% of ACV for moderate damage
diminished_value.eligibility: Not for total losses (already compensated at ACV); newer vehicles (<5 years) with significant damage; must show value loss (appraisal, comparable sales)

liability_investigation.fault_determination: Police report (not binding but influential); witness statements; physical evidence (point of impact, debris, skid marks, dash cam, surveillance footage); applicable traffic laws
liability_investigation.comparative_negligence: States vary: pure comparative (fault % reduces recovery, can collect even if 99% at fault), modified comparative (bar at 50-51% at fault), contributory (1% at fault = no recovery)
liability_investigation.subrogation: Insurer pays insured, then pursues other party's insurance to recover; insured may have deductible waived if full recovery; arbitration if insurers dispute fault
liability_investigation.uninsured_motorist: UM coverage; if at-fault party has no insurance; insured collects from own policy; may require police report; UIM if other party underinsured (limits too low)
liability_investigation.hit_and_run: Uninsured motorist covers if state allows (some require UM to apply); collision coverage always applies (with deductible); police report typically required within 24 hours

first_notice_of_loss.fnol_intake: Date/time/location of loss; description of what happened; parties involved; police/fire report number; injuries; immediate needs (emergency services, housing)
first_notice_of_loss.coverage_verification: Policy in force on date of loss; coverage applicable to peril; limits available; deductible; prior claims history (affects renewal, not current claim unless fraud)
first_notice_of_loss.assignment: Route to appropriate adjuster; property, auto, liability, CAT (catastrophe team for major events); complexity level (simple desk review vs field inspection)
first_notice_of_loss.initial_contact: 24-48 hour contact requirement in most states; set expectations (process, timeline, documentation needed); authorize emergency mitigation if needed

investigation.documentation: Photos/video of damage; measurements; receipts for emergency expenses; contractor estimates; medical records (injury claims); employment records (loss of income)
investigation.recorded_statements: Optional but common; insured, claimant, witnesses; document version of events before memories fade; can be used if story changes later; must inform being recorded
investigation.site_inspection: In-person inspection for significant claims; verify damage matches reported; identify pre-existing damage; take measurements; create estimate
investigation.expert_referrals: Engineer (structural damage assessment), appraiser (contents valuation), medical examiner (injury claims), fire investigator (cause and origin), accountant (business interruption)

reserve_setting.initial_reserve: Estimate of claim payout; based on preliminary information; includes indemnity (payment to insured) + expenses (adjuster, engineer, legal)
reserve_setting.reserve_adequacy: Update as information develops; supplements, additional damage, liability assessment changes; under-reserving = poor planning; over-reserving = ties up capital
reserve_setting.ibnr: Incurred But Not Reported; reserves for claims not yet filed but expected (latent injury, delayed reporting); company-wide, not claim-specific

payment_types.rcv: Replacement Cost Value; pays full cost to replace with new of like kind and quality; no depreciation; common for dwelling, sometimes contents
payment_types.acv: Actual Cash Value; RCV minus depreciation; for older roofs, vehicles, sometimes contents; payment at time of loss
payment_types.agreed_value: Pre-agreed amount (classic cars, artwork); no depreciation dispute; pays agreed value if total loss
payment_types.functional_replacement: Replace with less expensive but functionally equivalent; ornate plaster = drywall if policy allows; reduces payout

settlement.partial_payments: ACV up front; hold depreciation until repairs complete; or progress payments during construction; retain 10% until final inspection (ensure quality)
settlement.release: Full and final release of all claims related to loss; sign to receive final payment; cannot reopen claim after signing (except for hidden damage provisions)
settlement.mortgage_clause: If mortgage on property, check to mortgagee and insured jointly; mortgagee endorses to release funds; protects lender's interest; insured can't collect and not repair

disputes.appraisal: Policy provision; if disagree on amount of loss (not coverage), either party can demand; each selects appraiser, appraisers select umpire, majority rules; binding
disputes.public_adjuster: Insured hires PA (3-15% of settlement); PA represents insured, creates estimate, negotiates; can increase payout but fee reduces net; useful for complex claims or if overwhelmed
disputes.bad_faith: Insurer unfairly denies, delays, or underpays claim; varies by state; penalties can include policy limits + consequential damages + punitive damages + attorney fees; serious allegations
disputes.department_of_insurance: File complaint with state regulator; DOI investigates; can result in fines for insurer, required payment; free to consumer; insurer takes seriously

business_interruption.coverage: Lost income during shutdown due to covered loss (fire, storm); pays net income + continuing expenses (payroll, rent, loan payments); requires direct physical loss
business_interruption.calculation: Historical financials (last 12 months); project lost revenue during shutdown; subtract non-continuing expenses (COGS, commissions); add continuing expenses; monthly indemnity
business_interruption.extended_period: 30-360 days after reopening to return to normal revenue; business doesn't immediately bounce back; policy must include extended period of indemnity
business_interruption.civil_authority: Shutdown due to government order; requires physical damage in area; pandemic exclusions common after COVID (communicable disease exclusions added)

large_loss_commercial.cat_claims: Catastrophe events (hurricanes, wildfires, tornadoes, hail storms); adjuster deployed to area; hundreds or thousands of claims; prioritize by severity; temporary housing, emergency repairs
large_loss_commercial.equipment_breakdown: Boilers, HVAC, electrical systems, computers; sudden mechanical/electrical failure; requires inspection, cause determination; may need engineer
large_loss_commercial.construction_defect: Builder's risk, CGL claims; defective workmanship claims; construction defect litigation; long-tail (claims arise years later); complex

liability_claims.premises_liability: Slip and fall, inadequate security, dog bite; injured party claims negligent maintenance or security; must prove duty, breach, causation, damages
liability_claims.bodily_injury: Medical bills, lost wages, pain and suffering; settle or litigate; demand package (medical records, bills, lost wage verification); 3× medical specials rule of thumb for settlement
liability_claims.property_damage_liability: Damage to other's property; car accident, tree falls on neighbor's house, contractor damages customer property; repair or replace; diminished value claims
liability_claims.attorney_representation: Insurer provides defense under liability coverage; defense costs outside policy limits (duty to defend); insured cannot settle without insurer consent (duty to indemnify)

types.staff_adjuster: Employee of insurance company; resident license in home state; reciprocity or temporary licenses for other states
types.independent_adjuster: Contractor; works for adjusting firms; deployed for CAT or daily claims; needs license in each state worked (or reciprocity); more flexible
types.public_adjuster: Represents policyholders; negotiates with insurers; 3-15% fee; separate license; fiduciary duty to insured; some states heavily regulate

adjuster_licensing.requirements: Pre-licensing education (20-40 hours varies by state); state exam (pass 70-75%); background check; surety bond ($5k-$50k for PA); E&O insurance; CE (continuing education 24 hours biannually typical)
adjuster_licensing.catastrophe_licenses: Emergency adjuster license; some states waive requirements during declared emergencies; 90-day temporary license; allows out-of-state adjusters to help

claims_regulations.prompt_payment: State laws; 30-90 days to pay or deny after proof of loss submitted; penalties for late payment (interest, attorney fees, bad faith damages)
claims_regulations.unfair_claims_practices: Cannot misrepresent coverage, alter policy terms, fail to investigate, lowball without justification; state insurance codes define; penalties and fines
claims_regulations.privacy: GLBA (Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act); protect customer financial information; privacy policy disclosure; secure data; cannot share without consent
claims_regulations.fraud_detection: SIU (Special Investigations Unit); red flags (inconsistent statements, prior claims history, staged accidents, inflated estimates); refer suspicious claims; cooperate with law enforcement

estimating_software.xactimate: Property claims; market leader; sketch tools; line-item database; Xactimate price list (regional); mobile app XactContents
estimating_software.ccc_one: Auto claims; total loss valuation (market comparable sales); repair estimating; photo estimating (AI damage recognition)
estimating_software.mitchell: Auto claims; estimating, total loss, subrogation; WorkCenter platform
estimating_software.symbility: Property claims; competitor to Xactimate; mobile estimating; cloud-based

claims_management_systems.guidewire: Enterprise claims management; large insurers; configurable workflows; integrates with estimating tools
claims_management_systems.duck_creek: Policy and claims platform; mid to large carriers
claims_management_systems.applied_epic: Independent agency management; smaller carriers and MGAs

investigation_tools.iso_claims_search: Database of prior claims; detect fraud (multiple claims same person); underwriting tool
investigation_tools.carfax_autocheck: Vehicle history; prior accidents, title brands, odometer; total loss valuation
investigation_tools.moisture_meters: Pinless or pin-type; measure moisture content in materials; verify dry before closing water claim
investigation_tools.thermal_imaging: FLIR camera; detect moisture in walls, roof leaks, HVAC issues, electrical hot spots
